title: PLA Surges to 15 Sorties on Navy 77th Anniversary — 14 of 15 Enter SW ADIZ; Re-Escalation from April 22 Pullback
detail: Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense reported 15 PLA aircraft sorties and 5 PLAN vessels plus 1 official ship operating around Taiwan as of 06:00 local time on April 23, 2026 — a significant re-escalation from the 6-sortie pullback of April 22. Of the 15 sorties, 14 entered Taiwan's southwestern ADIZ — a concentrated southwestern penetration pattern. The spike coincides directly with the PLA Navy's 77th founding anniversary (founded April 23, 1949), a date the PLA routinely uses for symbolic military signaling. The April 23 jump to 15 sorties disrupts the expected post-spike de-escalation arc: Liaoning carrier transit April 20 (5 sorties) → 24-sortie surge April 21 (highest non-exercise count since Justice Mission-2025) → 6-sortie pullback April 22 → 15-sortie anniversary spike April 23. Taiwan's armed forces deployed combat air patrols, naval ships, and coastal missile systems in response. With 21 days remaining before the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing (May 14-15), the PLA anniversary spike signals Beijing will maintain military pressure throughout the pre-summit diplomatic period rather than showing restraint to ease negotiations. Analysts note that the concentrated southwestern ADIZ focus (14 of 15 sorties) differs from the multi-sector pattern of the April 21 surge and more closely resembles baseline elevated-pressure operations rather than exercise rehearsal geometry.

media.caption: Taiwan MND reports 15 PLA sorties and 5 PLAN vessels on April 23 — re-escalation on PLA Navy 77th anniversary, 14 of 15 entering SW ADIZ
15 PLA aircraft sorties detected around Taiwan — Southwest ADIZ focus on PLA Navy founding anniversary

title: PLA Navy Marks 77th Founding Anniversary — Record 40+ Vessels Opened to Public in 10+ Cities; Beijing Projects Naval Power
detail: The Chinese People's Liberation Army Navy celebrated its 77th founding anniversary (April 23, 1949) on April 23, 2026 with record-scale open-ship events: 40+ active-duty vessels opened to the public across 10+ coastal cities including Dalian, Qingdao, Shanghai, Ningbo, Xiamen, Guangzhou, and Sanya — the largest and most diverse such event in PLA Navy history. The vessels on display included guided-missile destroyers (Urumqi Hull 118, Qiqihar Hull 121), guided-missile frigates (Weifang Hull 550, Wuhu Hull 539), minesweepers, missile boats, amphibious landing ships, comprehensive supply ships, and hospital ships. PRC state media covered the anniversary with messaging emphasizing the PLAN's 'growing role in safeguarding peace' — a direct contrast to the simultaneous 15-sortie military pressure operation around Taiwan conducted on the same day. Analysts note the dual messaging on the anniversary: outward-facing naval display of soft power (public open-ship events) combined with a military pressure signal to Taiwan (15 sorties, 14 entering SW ADIZ) reflects Beijing's consistent dual-track approach of narrative management and coercive signaling.

media.caption: PLA Navy 77th anniversary: record 40+ vessels opened to public in 10+ cities — record-scale soft power display simultaneous with 15-sortie pressure around Taiwan
